pilot_banner.title: Pilot — limited beta.
pilot_banner.body: This build operates on real client data. Triage feedback via the Feedback button.

feedback.title: Feedback to ops
feedback.context_note: Auto-included: current route, session id, browser. Don't paste client data — narrate in your own words.
feedback.description_placeholder: Describe what went wrong, what you expected, and any signals (toast text, error code, behavior).
feedback.trying_label: What were you trying to do?
feedback.trying_placeholder: Optional. The flow you were following when this came up.

welcome_tour.title: Welcome to MP2.0
welcome_tour.step_pick_client_body: Use the picker in the topbar to choose a household. The stage centers on that client — AUM, accounts, goals, and risk.
welcome_tour.step_drill_treemap_body: Click any tile in the treemap to open the account or goal view. The right-side context panel shows source provenance for the values you see.
welcome_tour.step_review_doc_drop_body: Visit Review to drag-and-drop a folder of client docs. Extraction runs in the background; review the conflict cards before committing the household.

topbar.methodology_label: Methodology — how every score, bucket, and projection is computed

auth.role_unsupported: Your account is not configured for this console.

ctx.placeholder_body: Detail content lands in subsequent phases. The chrome scaffold is in place per the approved migration plan.

deferred.projections_r4: Projections + fan chart land in Phase R4.
deferred.history_r6: History snapshots arrive in Phase R6.

household.select_first: Select a client from the topbar to view their household.
household.missing_client: Client not found or you don't have access.

account.no_goals_in_account: This account has no goal allocations yet.

goal.missing_goal: Goal not found in this household.
goal.advanced_panels_pending: Allocation, projections, optimizer output, and rebalance moves arrive in Phase R4.

review.placeholder_description: Doc-drop and conflict resolution surface ships in Phase R7.

cma.placeholder_description: Analyst CMA tooling ships in Phase R9.

risk_slider.title: Goal risk
risk_slider.override_active_banner: Override active · Document rationale before saving
risk_slider.rationale_placeholder: Why are you overriding the system score? Capture context for the audit trail.
risk_slider.toast_saved: Override saved with rationale captured in audit log.
risk_slider.locked_tooltip: Your role cannot edit goal risk. Phase B will add Request access flow.
risk_slider.locked_description: Override capture requires the advisor:risk:write permission.
risk_slider.history_empty: No overrides on file. System score is in effect.

fan_chart.title: Projected outcome
fan_chart.no_data: No projection available — start value or horizon missing.
fan_chart.probability_callout: {{pct}}% chance of reaching target by year {{year}}

goal_allocation.no_holdings: No holdings on file for this goal.

moves.no_moves: No rebalance moves required — current matches ideal within rounding.

banner.bold: Most households are onboarded by uploading documents and conversation notes.
banner.body: The AI extraction handles the data entry. Use this form for edge cases, or when you have all the data ready to enter manually.

recovery.title: Resume in-progress household setup?
recovery.body: An unsaved wizard draft was found in this browser session.

commit.success_body: The household tree was created and a portfolio recommendation was generated.
commit.warn_blockers_body: {{count}} item(s) need to be resolved before a portfolio recommendation can be generated. Open the household to review.

step1.title: Who is the household?
step1.subtitle: Basic identification. Risk profile and goals come next.
step1.consent_label: Both members consent to a joint household view.
step1.consent_help: Joint households can share fees and reporting. Both members must sign a consent form before account opening.
step1.notes_placeholder: Anything an advisor should know — life events, preferences, prior history…

step2.title: Household risk profile
step2.subtitle: Four questions establish tolerance (T) and capacity (C). The household anchor is min(T, C) ÷ 2.
step2.q1_text: How do you see yourself: are you generally a person who is fully prepared to take risks, or do you try to avoid taking risks?
step2.q1_legend: 0 = avoid risk · 10 = fully prepared to take risks
step2.q2_text: When something unexpected and stressful happens, how do you typically respond?
step2.q3_text: Which of these are likely sources of stress for the household over the next 5 years? Select all that apply.
step2.q4_text: What is the household's capacity to take on investment risk?

step3.title: Accounts and goals
step3.subtitle: Add the household's accounts, then assign each goal's funding across them.
step3.missing_holdings_confirmed_label: No fund-level holdings to track for this account (e.g., GIC, single fund, cash).

step4.title: External holdings (optional)
step4.subtitle: Funds held outside Steadyhand. Asset percentages must sum to 100.
step4.empty: No external holdings on file. Click Add holding or skip.

step5.title: Review and commit
step5.subtitle: Confirm the household tree before committing. Audit event fires on success.
step5.preflight_allocation_mismatch: Account total {{account}} ≠ goal allocations {{allocated}}. Adjust legs in Step 3 so they sum to the account total.
step5.preflight_footer: These are advisor hints. The household will commit either way; you can resolve gaps after.

empty.no_clients: No clients yet. Onboard a household via doc-drop or the wizard.
empty.no_goals: This household has no goals yet.
empty.no_portfolio_run: No portfolio generated yet for this household.
empty.no_holdings: No holdings on file for this account.
empty.no_snapshots: No history yet for this household.
empty.backend_unavailable: Backend unavailable. Retry once the API is reachable.

errors.boundary_scoped: Error in {{scope}}. The rest of the console is still available.
errors.report_phase_b: Reporting channel coming in Phase B.

scaffold.title: v36 rewrite scaffold ready
scaffold.description: Engine modules + design tokens + i18n + a11y + telemetry are wired. The advisor console (TopBar / Stage / Context Panel / three-view) lands in subsequent phases per the approved migration plan.

realign.title: Goal realignment
realign.intro: Re-goaling re-labels dollars between goals. Underlying holdings don't move and no money changes accounts. The blended risk score for each account may shift; the audit trail captures every change.
realign.no_goals_for_account: No goals are linked to this account yet.
realign.legs_balanced: All accounts balanced. Apply to record before/after snapshots.
realign.legs_imbalanced: Each account's leg sums must equal its current value before Apply.
realign.confirm_body: After-state preserved. History tab shows both snapshots.
realign.revert_body: Before-state restored as a new history snapshot.

compare.intro: Side-by-side compare of two household snapshots. Δ shows the change relative to the before column.
compare.no_goals: No goals captured in this snapshot.

history.restore_success_body: A new history row was added for the restore action.

review_route.title: Review queue
review_route.subtitle: Drop documents to start a new household review, or pick an in-flight workspace from the queue.
review_route.queue_empty: No in-flight reviews. Drop documents above to start one.
review_route.select_prompt: Select a workspace from the queue or start a new one above.

docdrop.title: Drop documents to start a review
docdrop.intro: Documents and conversation notes flow through the AI extraction pipeline. The advisor reviews structured facts in the next screen — raw text never persists.
docdrop.upload_success_body: Processing has started. Watch progress in the review queue.
docdrop.session_expired_body: Sign in again — we kept your workspace label and file list so you can re-pick the documents and resume.
docdrop.draft_restored_body_one: Re-pick the {{count}} file you dropped before for "{{label}}" to continue.
docdrop.draft_restored_body_other: Re-pick the {{count}} files you dropped before for "{{label}}" to continue.
docdrop.draft_recovered_body_one: Pick {{count}} file with the same name to continue.
docdrop.draft_recovered_body_other: Pick {{count}} files with the same names to continue.

docdrop.too_large_body_other: {{count}} files are over {{limit_mb}}MB and were skipped: {{names}}
docdrop.duplicate_body_one: {{count}} file was already in your list: {{names}}
docdrop.duplicate_body_other: {{count}} files were already in your list: {{names}}
docdrop.retry_failed_body_one: {{count}} file didn't make it. Retry just this one without re-picking.
docdrop.retry_failed_body_other: {{count}} files didn't make it. Retry just these without re-picking.

review.subtitle: Status: {{status}} · Origin: {{origin}}
review.commit_success_body: The household tree was created and an audit event recorded.
review.uncommit_success_body: The household was removed; you can edit and re-commit when ready.
review.synthetic_badge_aria: This workspace uses synthetic data for development and demo only.
review.manual_entry_success_body: Reconcile will skip this doc. Fill the missing fields by hand in the review state. (Previous failure: {{previous_code}}.)
review.manual_entry_error: Could not mark document for manual entry.

failure_code.bedrock_token_limit: This document exceeded the AI extraction output budget. Retry once; if it still fails, mark as manual entry and fill fields by hand.
failure_code.bedrock_non_json: AI extraction returned an unparseable response. Retry, or mark as manual entry.
failure_code.bedrock_schema_mismatch: AI extraction returned data in an unexpected shape. Mark as manual entry; fields are unrecoverable from this document.
failure_code.bedrock_unknown: AI extraction failed for an unspecified reason. Retry or mark as manual entry.
failure_code.bedrock_unavailable: AI extraction service is temporarily unavailable. Retry in a moment.
failure_code.WorkerStalled: The extraction worker stalled mid-job. Retry; if it stalls repeatedly, contact ops.
failure_code.ImproperlyConfigured: Storage or model configuration is invalid. Contact ops.
failure_code.OSError: Storage I/O error. Retry; if it repeats, contact ops.

worker_health.offline_body_one: {{count}} document is queued. Contact ops if this persists.

conflict.heading: Same-source disagreements
conflict.rationale_placeholder: e.g. KYC is canonical source of record per canon §11.4 — DOB on KYC supersedes statement.
conflict.evidence_ack: I've reviewed the source evidence for each candidate.
conflict.add_to_bulk: Add this resolution to a bulk batch
conflict.bulk_bar_body: Apply one rationale + evidence acknowledgement to all selected conflicts.
conflict.defer_body: Section approval will proceed without resolving this. The conflict will auto-resurface if new evidence arrives.
conflict.deferred_toast_body: {{label}} marked deferred. Re-surfaces if new evidence arrives.
conflict.resurfaced_body: New evidence arrived since this was deferred — please reconsider.

doc_detail.no_contributed: This document didn't contribute any current facts.

cma.forbidden_body: The CMA Workbench is reserved for the financial-analyst role. Sign in with an analyst account to publish capital-market assumptions.

cma.no_active_for_draft: No active CMA snapshot to copy from.
cma.no_snapshots: No CMA snapshots on file. Seed default CMA via the management command.

cma.frontier_points_summary: {{efficient}} efficient frontier points · {{funds}} individual fund anchors

methodology.heading: Methodology
methodology.intro: Every score, projection, and recommendation in MP2.0 traces back to one of the formulas below. Each section names the inputs, the formula, the variables, and a worked example. Use this page to ramp up advisors, support compliance reviews, and explain individual recommendations to clients.
methodology.footer: MP2.0 methodology · canon v2.8 · client-facing labels lock to: Cautious / Conservative-balanced / Balanced / Balanced-growth / Growth-oriented.

s1.title: Household risk profile
s1.summary: The household risk score combines a tolerance score (T, 0–100) derived from three behavioral questions with a capacity score (C, 0–100) derived from one financial-capacity question. The household profile is the lower of the two, snapped to the canon 1–5 scale.
s1.formula:
T = 0.5·Q1 + 0.3·Q2 + 0.2·Q3
C = Q4
household_score (1–5) = bucket(min(T, C))
s1.var_q3: Count of stressors the client flagged as material
s1.worked: Hayes household: Q1 = 5, Q2 = B (moderate), Q3 = 1 stressor (career), Q4 = B → T = 45, C = 50 → min(T, C) = 45 → bucket → Balanced (canon score 3).
s1.footnote: Time horizon is NOT a component of the household risk score (canon §4.3d). Horizon constrains specific goals, not the household profile.

s2.title: Anchor
s2.summary: The anchor is the household-level reference point each goal departs from when its individual score is computed. It is the lower of T and C, scaled to the 0–50 internal range used for goal-level computation.
s2.worked: Hayes household: anchor = min(45, 50) / 2 = 22.5. Every Hayes goal starts from anchor 22.5 before importance and size shifts apply.

s3.title: Goal-level risk score
s3.summary: Each goal's risk score starts from the household anchor, shifts by importance (Need / Want / Wish / Unsure) and shifts by size (this goal as a share of household AUM), then snaps to the canon 1–5 scale.
s3.formula:
importance_shift = imp_shift(tier)
size_shift = size_shift(goal_amount / household_aum)
score (1–5) = bucket(anchor + importance_shift + size_shift)
s3.var_tier: Need / Want / Wish / Unsure (mapped from the goal's necessity_score)
s3.var_size_shift: Larger share of AUM ⇒ more conservative (negative shift); smaller share ⇒ more growth-oriented (positive shift)
s3.var_bucket: Snap the shifted value to one of the five canon descriptors
s3.worked: Hayes Retirement Goal: anchor 22.5, tier = Need (−10), 47% of AUM (size_shift = −2) → resolves to canon Conservative-balanced (score 2). Horizon 32 years applies no effective cap; no advisor override on file.
s3.footnote: Engineers reading this: the engine internally computes a 0–50 intermediate (`Goal_50`) before snapping to canon 1–5. Goal_50 is never displayed to advisors and is not part of any API surface (locked decision #6). For this example, Goal_50 = 22.5 + (−10) + (−2) = 10.5 → bucket-snap → Conservative-balanced.

s4.title: Horizon cap per goal
s4.summary: Long-dated goals can sustain more variability than near-term goals. The horizon cap sets the maximum descriptor a goal may have based on its time horizon. The system-derived score is min(uncapped, cap).

s5.title: Effective bucket
s5.summary: The effective descriptor is what the optimizer actually uses for the goal's allocation. Advisor overrides (with documented rationale) take precedence over the system-derived value; otherwise the effective descriptor is the lower of the uncapped score and the horizon cap.
s5.detail: An override carries the advisor identity, timestamp, before-and-after canon descriptor, and a free-text rationale (minimum 10 characters), all written to the immutable audit log. Junior-advisor accounts may have the override capability gated; the locked surface displays a request-access affordance instead of the slider.

s6.title: Sleeve mix
s6.summary: Each canon score maps to a target equity percentage and an optimizer-determined building-block fund mix. The optimizer runs the efficient frontier over the eight-fund universe and returns the optimal allocation for that score's expected return + variance constraints.
s6.worked: Choi Travel goal at canon Balanced (score 3): equity_pct ≈ 49% (calibration curve at the bucket midpoint, BUCKET_REPRESENTATIVE_SCORE[3] = 25). Optimizer mixes Steadyhand Income, Equity, Global, and Savings funds; returns a frontier-optimal point at the score's μ/σ.
s6.footnote: The codebase's `SLEEVE_REF_POINTS` table is calibration-only — it anchors the parity tests against the v36 mockup's worked examples. Production sleeve mixes always come from the optimizer (canon §4.1).

s7.title: Lognormal projections
s7.summary: Goal-level projections use a lognormal distribution parameterized by the score-derived μ (expected return) and σ (volatility). External holdings carry a stiffer drift penalty than Steadyhand-managed sleeves to reflect realistic deviation from advisor-recommended allocations.
s7.formula:
μ_ideal(score) = 0.030 + equity_pct(score) × 0.045
σ_ideal(score) = 0.030 + equity_pct(score) × 0.190
μ_current(score, ext=False) = μ_ideal × 0.92
μ_current(score, ext=True)  = μ_ideal × 0.85
σ_current(score, ext=False) = σ_ideal × 1.08
σ_current(score, ext=True)  = σ_ideal × 1.15
s7.detail: Tier-aware percentile bands: Need-tier goals show P10/P90, Want-tier goals show P5/P95, Wish-tier goals show P2.5/P97.5. The probability of meeting target at horizon is computed as P(end_value ≥ target), surfaced as the “% chance” callout on the fan chart.
s7.worked: Thompson Retirement goal at canon Balanced-growth (score 4, representative midpoint 35): equity_pct ≈ 67% → μ_ideal = 0.030 + 0.67 × 0.045 ≈ 6.0%. For an off-target Steadyhand sleeve mix: μ_current = μ_ideal × 0.92 ≈ 5.5%. For an off-target external holding: μ_current = μ_ideal × 0.85 ≈ 5.1%. The drift is what the fan chart visualizes between plan-baseline and what-if curves.
s7.footnote: External-holdings adjustment is a projection-time penalty only (μ × 0.85, σ × 1.15). The canon §4.6a risk-tolerance dampener is deferred to Phase B awaiting confirmed input parameters.

s8.title: Rebalancing moves
s8.summary: Whenever current allocation differs from ideal, the engine emits a buy/sell list rounded to the nearest $100. The residual cents from rounding are absorbed into the largest deficit-side move so total buys equal total sells exactly.
s8.formula:
Δ(fund) = (ideal_pct − current_pct) × goal_total_dollars
round(Δ) = nearest $100 (residual to largest deficit)
invariant: Σbuys == Σsells
s8.worked: Choi Education goal, $80,000 total: 4-pp shortfall in Steadyhand Equity, 4-pp surplus in Steadyhand Savings. Δ = ±$3,200; clean $100 rounding; residual $0; sells == buys.

s9.title: Goal realignment
s9.summary: Re-goaling is a label change only. The advisor reassigns dollars between goals at the account level; underlying holdings do not move. No taxable event, no fund trades, no money movement — only a metadata change to which goal each account-dollar is intended to fund.
s9.detail: Realignment opens a compare screen showing before-and-after goal aggregates and per-goal deltas. The advisor confirms or reverts; either choice creates an append-only HouseholdSnapshot tagged with the action and the advisor identity.
s9.footnote: Vocabulary discipline (canon §6.3a): use “re-goaling”, “goal realignment”, “re-label dollars between goals”. Never use “reallocation”, “transfer”, or “move money” to describe this — those terms imply trades that do not occur.

s10.title: Archive snapshots
s10.summary: Every state-changing action that affects a household's planning model creates an append-only HouseholdSnapshot. Snapshots capture a deep clone of the household plus precomputed aggregates (AUM split, asset mix, fund mix, blended risk score, anchor) for fast Compare-screen rendering.
s10.trigger_realignment_desc: Re-goaling action that changed account-to-goal mapping
s10.trigger_cash_in_desc: External funds added to a household account
s10.trigger_cash_out_desc: Funds withdrawn from a household account
s10.trigger_re_link_desc: External-holdings record updated against a goal
s10.trigger_override_desc: Advisor saved a goal-risk override with rationale
s10.trigger_restore_desc: Advisor restored from a prior snapshot (creates a new snapshot tagged restore; does not rewind state)
s10.footnote: Snapshots are append-only via Django model `save()` guards plus a Postgres BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE trigger. There is no admin path to mutate or delete a snapshot; the only “undo” is to take a new restore action.

realign_preview.title: What's about to change
realign_preview.empty_body: Adjust a leg above to see the projected per-goal and per-account-leg shifts.
